step_by_step_reasoning: The architecture is split into ingestion, transformation, and distribution phases. In the ingestion phase, files arrive from external systems (UserCompanyDataStream) and are placed in S3 for storage, while their metadata is queued in SQS. A work manager Lambda processes these SQS messages to store tracking state in RDS. In the transformation phase, an EMR trigger Lambda reads RDS state to run Spark transformations on EMR, reading raw S3 files and outputting optimized files to another S3 bucket, updating RDS upon completion. In the distribution phase, a second work manager Lambda initiates a massive fan-out execution of uploader Lambdas. These uploaders pull files from the output S3 bucket, upload them to external Ad Networks under configure-based rate-limits, and report status back to RDS asynchronously through a buffering SQS queue and a batching status writer Lambda to avoid Postgres connection exhaustion.

graph.name: Nielsen Marketing Cloud DataOut Platform
graph.notes: Handles 250 billion daily events scaling dynamically using Serverless Lambdas and Spark on EMR. Protects downstream systems from API overload using queue-based buffering and smart rate-limiting.

nodes.name: Data Files
nodes.notes: External client data source emitting raw files.

nodes.name: Postgres RDS
nodes.notes: Central state database managing processing workflows.

nodes.name: EMR Scheduler Lambda
nodes.notes: Coordinates EMR jobs based on database state.

nodes.name: Output Bucket
nodes.notes: Stores transformed and formatted target files.

nodes.name: Upload Coordinator Lambda
nodes.notes: Performs fan-out triggers of uploaders based on RDS status.

nodes.name: Target Uploader Lambdas
nodes.notes: Runs concurrently up to thousands of instances to push files.

nodes.name: Buffer SQS Queue
nodes.notes: Prevents RDS DB connection exhaustion by queuing status reports.

nodes.name: Status Database Writer
nodes.notes: Pulls batches from SQS to update RDS states.

edges.notes: Polls for files ready to process
